entry.title: SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
entry.content:
Welcome to the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Our fully-fledged cloud ERP is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and offers innovation and enhancements beyond measure. But even the most scalable cloud ERP would only be half as efficient without a seamless user experience. Therefore, we keep enhancing our product based on your feedback to offer you a next-level user experience so that you can thrive in today’s work environment. It is my pleasure to show you just how we do that in this release. Spoiler alert: Generative AI is involved. 😉

Check out my video below to get a first glimpse into what’s coming in the area of SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402:

Horizon Theme

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2208, we introduced the Horizon visual theme to offer you a fresh and modern SAP Fiori experience and to help you get your job done faster, more intuitively, and with better outcomes. Taking your feedback to heart, we’ve already made continuous improvements to the design in past releases. With this new release, we now implemented even more changes such as:

  • White input fields instead of gray,
  • Clearly identifiable buttons,
  • Improved contrast,
  • Reduced header size,
  • And more.

Read more about this in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Figure 1: The darker background on the My Home is only one of many design improvements to the Horizon visual theme.

Spaces and Pages

As announced in my previous blog post, the groups setup on the SAP Fiori launchpad is now officially outdated; spaces and pages are the only available layout option as of this release (read more in this note). Switching to spaces and pages has now become crucial to avoid a blank screen on the SAP Fiori launchpad–and note, the "switch" does not happen automatically, but has to be done manually. Read this blog post by Sylvie Charpentier on how to efficiently switch to spaces and pages. We are granting a grace period until June 2024 to all customers who haven’t switched to spaces and pages yet. During this grace period, there’s no guarantee that the group-based content is up to date. At the end of this grace period, SAP will change the settings in development and test systems first, and eventually also in production systems to display spaces and pages as default without the option for administrators and business users to switch back to groups. The administrators of affected customers will be informed about these changes in due time.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, we ship a new functionality to merge spaces and pages which can be used to e.g., reduce the number of duplicate apps, pages, or spaces for users. This can also be helpful if you want to enrich SAP-delivered spaces or pages with customer-defined content.

Figure 2: “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager” before merging.

Figure 3: View after merging “Accounts Payable Accountant” and “Accounts Payable Manager”.

My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Switching to spaces and pages will not only give you a new layout option to structure the access to apps following work processes in an intuitive and efficient way; it also enables you to use the My Home entry page in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. Users benefit from a personalized interface tailored to their needs, with important to-dos and insights readily accessible. Designed for efficiency across devices, the My Home ensures quick task execution through intuitive app organization. With the 2402 release, we offer an increased level of personalization to make the user experience even more straightforward. This includes:

  • The harmonization of blank state,
  • Consistent group creation via drag and drop,
  • A total re-design of the app section,
  • And an increased language support for system news.

For new customers, spaces and pages and My Home are the default and you will benefit from them immediately.

Figure 4: Discover the latest enhancements to the My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402. Click on the image to watch the video.

Collaborative ERP

The My Home in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition offers a new collaboration feature with Microsoft Teams. Based on contact information in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can now directly trigger a Microsoft Teams chat or voice/video call and start collaborating.

Figure 5: The integration between My Home and Microsoft Teams allows you to easily collaborate across apps. Click on the image to watch the video.

And there’s more: Another feature of the collaborative ERP is now generally available! We enable you to share your most relevant business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats. Through the cards, recipients of the message can directly edit the data in the collaborative stage view in Microsoft Teams embedded in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Figure 6: Share business data using cards in Microsoft Teams chats and start collaborating. Click the image to watch the video.

SAP Collaboration Manager

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2302, we introduced the SAP Collaboration Manager. This cloud-based, innovative collaboration feature replaced the human-to-human chat function of the former SAP CoPilot in the three-system landscape and offers a seamlessly integrated collaboration experience directly inside the SAP Fiori launchpad, increasing overall user efficiency. Since then, we’ve been continuously enhancing the SAP Collaboration Manager to offer more capabilities such as creating chats and inviting your co-workers to collaborate without any system switch over, taking screenshots and annotating or highlighting them, and linking your chat to any screen for reference. In this release, we offer even more enhancements:

  • Add My Home “Insights” cards,
  • Mark notes and chats as “Read” or “Unread”,
  • Add notes and chats to “Favorites”.

Figure 7: The “Add Insights Cards” function allows you to provide convenient access to analytical information, lists, graphs, and associated apps.

SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects

As of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, developers can work with SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio (SAP BAS) to create variants of standard applications. In an adaptation project, you can extend the original code to define changes that fulfill the specific business requirements of different business roles, user groups, organizational units, and countries. For example, you might want to simplify and streamline a specific process by hiding fields not required by casual users, visualizing data as a chart, or changing a responsive table to a grid table to make information easier to process. After deployment, both the original application and your adapted application exist in parallel and can be made available to the relevant users through uniquely name application tiles in SAP Fiori launchpad/My Home.

Figure 8: You can now create SAPUI5 Adaptation Projects in SAP Business Application Studio to generate app variants. Click the image to watch the video.

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402

In my first paragraph of this blog post, I promised to talk about generative AI–so, let’s talk about SAP’s brand-new, natural language genAI copilot, Joule. Joule is a comprehensive solution designed to understand the complexities of your business processes and will completely revolutionize how you interact with the system. It provides transparent, role-based access to relevant processes, making interactions intuitive and seamless. By enabling natural conversations between users and systems, Joule enhances productivity and delivers valuable insights. The three supported conversational patterns are:

  • Navigational: Joule helps users find required functionalities within SAP systems,
  • Transactional: Joule assists users in completing tasks,
  • Informational: Joule provides insights based on SAP Help documentation data.

With SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402, Joule is taking off through an Early Adopter Care program and will be available for selected customers for initial testing. Read more about Joule and other AI innovations in this blog post by Syed Talal Hussain.

Figure 9: SAP’s generative AI copilot Joule revolutionizes how you interact with the system. Click the image to watch the video.

Customer Influence Page

Our top priority has always been ensuring a seamless and straightforward user experience. Reacting to your feedback and requirements is the key to achieving this. The main input channel where we receive your requirements is the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, User Experience and Cross-Topics customer influence page. To make sure we collect all your requests, we emphasize the importance of the channel and increase the threshold to 10 to get a better focus. We review permanently and stop all improvement requests that are older than 18 months and have not (yet) met the current voting threshold. Based on UX importance, here is a status report of features that were delivered and that are still in process/planning stage:

  • S4HC Home page personalization: User CB ID to be shown under “User Account” menu (19 votes), 2nd most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release
  • Adapt UI – Enable ability to set field properties (i.e., mandatory, visible) (15 votes), 4th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Can be achieved through UI Adaptation Projects
  • Upload custom logo without having to use UI Theme Designer (2TV) (13 votes), 6th most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Planned with next release (Q3/2024)
  • Enable a download/upload of the My Home app tile information (11 votes), 11th  most-voted improvement request in the session
    • Delivered with current release

Figure 10: Our customer influence page is the main channel to request requirements.

When I said we are offering next-level user experience with this new release, I wasn’t promising too much. Get more details about the innovations and enhancements mentioned in this blog post and much, much more in Thomas Reiss’s blog post.

Feel free to drop a kudo if you liked this blog post. Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our new community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts. Looking forward to the next release!

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn and X.

📽️ Watch Our Live Sessions

In January 2024, we hosted a series of 19 compelling live sessions to highlight the exciting innovations shipped with the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release. Missed the live sessions? No problem! Take advantage of the entire series or pick and choose the sessions most relevant to you on demand.

 

🎙️ Inside SAP S/4HANA Cloud

There is no customer success without project success and product success! Leverage the unique knowledge and expertise of SAP S/4HANA Cloud experts, partners, and customers, sharing their SAP S/4HANA Cloud implementation project best practices and lessons learned – anywhere, anytime. In this podcast, we give you all ingredients to get to the next level and make your SAP S/4HANA Cloud project a success. Subscribe now and benefit from the shared knowledge!

 

💻openSAP Microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud

Our openSAP microlearnings for SAP S/4HANA Cloud offer an exciting new learning opportunity. What began with a small batch of 20 videos, has now become a channel with more than 50 microlearnings that have generated over 20,000 views. Today, we cover multiple lines of business such as finance, manufacturing, and supply chain management, and key technology topics like Master Data Management, extensibility, SAP User Experience, and upgrade management. We are continuously adding new microlearnings to our channel, so make sure you check them out.

 

🗣️ Your Voice Matters

You want to learn more and actively engage with SAP subject matter experts on SAP S/4HANA Cloud topics? We have just the right place for you! Join our interactive community where we bring together customers, partners, and SAP experts with a clear mission: To engage with one another about best practices and product solutions. We invite you to explore the ‘one-stop shop’ as the central place for all resources, tools, content questions, answers and to connect with experts who will guide you through your journey to the intelligent, sustainable enterprise.

 

🔎Find All of Our Assets

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

 

🎯Where to Find More Information:

  • Read up on the highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402 release here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/highlights-of-the-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-2402-release/ba-p/13587480
  • Curious about more SAP UX innovations in public cloud products? Read up here: https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/sap-ux-innovations-in-public-cloud-products-joule-ai-entry-points-apple/ba-p/13594954
  • Need more information on how to switch to spaces and pages? Find it here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/deactivation-of-groups-in-the-sap-fiori-launchpad-in-sap-s-4hana-cloud/ba-p/13578088 and here: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/how-to-efficiently-switch-from-groups-to-spaces-and-pages/ba-p/13604299

entry.title: To our Partners – SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Projects open for registration until March 08
entry.content:
The first cycle of the 2024 SAP Customer Engagement Initiative (CEI) is in full swing. The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you as an SAP customer or partner, to get early insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions. There has been a tremendous response in terms of customer registrations for different projects. 

SAP continuously works closely with many partners to build and innovate. Therefore, this is a specific call for partners to go through the projects and register to influence SAP products and future applications. This is your chance to make your voices heard. 

The projects will be open for registration until March 08, 2024.

Those are the projects that are explicitly looking for registrations from SAP Partners:

SAP S/4HANA & SAP S/4HANA Cloud

  • GenAI for Situation Handling
  • Planned New Cost Center Reporting with Review Booklets in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

Supply Chain Management

  • SAP PLM - Change Management for SAP EPD
  • SAP Service and Asset Management - Safety Technician Persona(WCM)

SAP Business Technology Platform

  • Hybrid integration with SAP Integration Suite
  • Learning from customers about their NEO migration journey and needs for user assistance
  • SAP Discovery Center Innovation – simplify the experience for technical users on the SAP BTP
  • Templates for No-Code Application Development with SAP Build Apps

Innovation Center Network

  • AI Business Agents with SAP BTP: An Exploration Project
  • HANA Ledger (private BETA): Enhancing Compliance and Traceability in Business Processes with BTP

SAP Globalization Services

  • Localization as a Self-Service for SAP S/4HANA Public Edition
  • Pilot customers for DMEE to DMEEX migration

AI

  • Smart Data Load - AI Supported Chart of Accounts Mapping

Data & Analytics

  • Migration to SAP HANA Cloud

Lifecycle Management

  • AI & GenAi Integration with Intelligent Scenario Lifecycle Management
  • Planned Enhancements to Maintenance Planner

SAP Learning

  • Customer Feedback Program for Learning SAP: discuss how to best listen to customers & partners
  • Learning SAP: let’s innovate together

Others

  • SAP Reference Architecture in SAP LeanIX

Visit the Customer Influence website on influence.sap.com to register.

Follow-up after registration: When you register for a project, you will be invited to an introductory call with the SAP project lead. At this point further participation is optional. Typically, all activities are governed by the Feedback Agreement with SAP.

Thank you in advance for your participation,

Your SAP Customer Engagement Initiative Team

entry.title: Invitation to join a test event for SAP Concur integration into SAP Funds and Grants Management
entry.content:
We recently released functionality that enables direct cost control between SAP Concur and SAP Financials Public Sector management. This functionality enables funds checking ("Do we have funds available?"), and funds reservations ("Reserve these funds for this traveler/spend"). Read more about it in this blog.

What is a test event?

A test event is an opportunity to get first hand experience with newly released or upcoming functionality. Through your testing, you can experiment with the functionality on familiar business processes, while having direct access to experts. You'll have the opportunity to share feedback and help shape our understanding of your needs. 

You'll bring your processes around budget control and funds reservations (also known as encumbrances, obligations, commitments, earmarking, etc), and we'll help you set up in our test environments. 

The test environments include access to Concur and SAP Financials.

Who can participate?

To ensure we can support all participants throughout the process, we have a limited number of spots available. Participants should be familiar with all of the following:

  • SAP Concur Expense
  • SAP Concur Request (optional)
  • SAP S/4 HANA Financials Public Sector Management

Participants will need to dedicate time to the event, participating in testing activities, kickoffs, and feedback sessions. 

Both partners and customers are invited participate.

When is the event?

The event is scheduled to occur April 23, 2024 through April 25, 2024 between the hours of 9 AM and 6 PM Central European Time. 

Registration will close March 4, 2024.

Where is the event?

This is a virtual event, so participants will be remote. The event will be facilitated by teams in Germany, so it will be held in Central European Time working hours.

What should I expect at the event?

Details will be shared with participants. High level plan is as follows:

  • Day 1:
    • Kickoff with guided demo of the product
    • Participants will be guided through access to the test environments
    • All day testing with open lines to Product, Engineering, and SME teams 
    • Closing feedback session
  • Day 2:
    • Daily kickoff with Q&A
    • All day testing with open lines to Product, Engineering, and SME teams 
    • Closing feedback session
  • Day 3:
    • Daily kickoff with Q&A
    • All day testing with open lines to Product, Engineering, and SME teams 
    • Closing feedback session

How can I register interest?

If you are interested in participating, please send an email with subject "I want to register for PSM Test" to: sapconcurintegration@sap.com and someone will be in touch to get more information.

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Now Shakes Hands with TLS 1.3!
entry.content:
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition has taken a step forward by introducing support for the latest TLS 1.3 protocol with 2402. This is not one of the fancy shiny feature you will see on keynotes. Instead, it's all about compatibility and keeping up with the times.

The best part? Customers don't need to change anything as this change is delivered and activated automatically or all SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition systems. It will probably go unnoticed for most users and many admins that in the background SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is ready to offer TLS 1.3, while still maintaining support for the proven TLS 1.2. For those forward-thinking TLS clients who speak the same language, SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is ready and will negotiate TLS1.3 with the TLS client.

entry.title: Switching to Spaces and Pages Experience Sharing - Deploying Spaces and Pages Best Practices
entry.content:
Introduction

In preparation of switching to Spaces and Pages from 2402 Release of SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, I have been working with many customers. In this series of blogs, I will share our common experience with you to make this transition smoother.

This blog discusses the best practices in deploying Spaces and Pages.

 

Short Summary of Spaces and Pages

SAP started rolling out Spaces and Pages in S/4HANA Cloud 2005 release. The intention is to flexibly organize the end user’s Fiori app layout by user’s personas in a hierarchy of spaces, pages, sections and apps. Depending on a business role, one or more spaces can be assigned.

To make spaces and pages working for customers, SAP released many preferred spaces and pages.  In other words, you can adopt these out of box spaces and pages assigned to SAP business role templates, or business roles derived from SAP business role templates, or those home-made business roles. The result is a consistent and almost maintenance free (checking and adapting at each major upgrade are still needed) Fiori app layout in your Launchpad. See my blog How to Use Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition for further explanation.

However, many customers didn’t bother to investigate or adopt this feature in the past years. They kept on using the classical group layout (one layer only: groups and apps). One customer I talked to has over 200 self-defined business roles; none of them has assigned spaces and pages. With the first day of switching to Spaces by Default, the end user’s screen will look like this (simulated by my colleague Marco Valencia), an empty MyHome:

 The Empty Fiori Launchpad (Spaces by Default, without Assigning Any Spaces and Pages to Business Roles)

Before switching, the business users are accustomed to see Fiori apps in the below Classical Homepage.

 The Classical Homepage Layout (Groups by Default Setting)

To avoid a surprise like above, and do the right things about Spaces and Pages, I am going to discuss the best practices starting from setting the Cloud Mindset.

 

Cloud Mindset

My colleague Jan Musil has a well written blog Updated 5 Golden Rules for implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud. The very first golden rule is as following:

  • Foster cloud mindset by adhering to Fit-to-Standard and Agile Deployment detailed in SAP Activate

To put it blankly, Cloud Mindset means adopting standard functions and features. Period.

I have been with SAP for 20+ years and involved in many customer projects. Been there, seen that and done that.  Last week, I had a call with one partner consultant.  He mentioned that while one selling point of SAP solutions is its flexibility or customization capability to fit all sorts of requirements from customers, the flip side of this flexibility is an increased difficulty in upgrading. Customers wrote millions of lines of codes without even realizing there are similar standard functions. The learning from the past on-premises solution deployment is to adhere to standard functions and features, especially for the cloud solutions when the upgrades happen so often and not under your control. You don’t have time and resources to deal with customizations every six months while the solutions are upgraded by SAP.

Talking about business roles, spaces and pages, there are even less justifications to create your own objects. Here is how authorization is designed in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition: Access to all Fiori Apps is controlled by Business Catalogs. Relevant Business Catalogs are grouped together to become Business Roles. Most Business Roles have relevant Spaces and Pages assigned based on personas.  When a user is assigned one or more relevant Business Roles, the user has access to those Fiori Apps following Spaces and Pages assignment (refer to my blog on this topic). That is the Fit-to-Standard approach.

 

Best Practices in Deploying Spaces and Pages

With the aforementioned “Cloud Mindset” in mind, let’s discuss the Best Practices in deploying Spaces and Pages.

Best Practice 1 – Using SAP Standard Business Roles and Associated Spaces and Pages for Your 90% Needs, And Using Favorites for Remaining 10% Needs

Arming yourself with the cloud mindset, you can easily make your business roles by copying from SAP predefined business role/space/page templates to fit most of your business needs. Let’s say that covers 90% of your needs.  There might be exceptions from predefined business role/space/page templates.  What are we going to do with the remaining 10%? The answer is using the Favorites section in My Home.

For example, I have a user needs to access Fiori App Create Billing Documents. From the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library, I found out two Business Catalogs grant access to this app. 

  • SAP_RFM_BC_STORE_SLSORDFUL_PC
  • SAP_SD_BC_BIL_DOC_PC

On top of that, there are multiple business roles, spaces and pages covering this Fiori App as the below figure shows.

 Access Info for Fiori App Create Billing Documents

Let’s assume this is not a so popular app, and only one business role Z_CREATE_BILLING_DOC includes the business catalog SAP_SD_BC_BIL_DOC_PC without any space and page assignment (see figure), because it is rarely used.

 Build One Custom Business Role with One Business Catalog

To make this app visible to the user, you just assign the business role Z_CREATE_BILLING_DOC to the user. Then ask the user to add the app in the Favorites section (see below). It is easy and quick.

 Add an App to the Favorites If Not Available in Standard Spaces and Pages

Another solution is to create a new space based on SAP standard spaces, for example Z_BR_BILLING_CLERK.  It depends on the effort of creation, and future maintenance. Personally, I would not go that route unless I have many apps like this one.

 Creating a Custom Space to Accommodate a Rarely Used App

 

Best Practice 2 – Adapting SAP Standard Spaces/Pages to be Your Spaces/Pages

Besides using SAP standard spaces and pages, the 2nd most popular approach is to adapt a standard one. There are a few benefits:

  1. You save time from designing a new space and its associated pages from scratch.
  2. You still have the flexibility to include only those apps you want in the custom spaces and pages.

Note: Different from predefined business role templates, when you create your own spaces and pages by copying from a predefined one, the system doesn't track the change of your spaces and pages. During the next upgrade, you need to check it out.

Let’s still use above custom space ZBR_BILLING_CLERK as an example. It is a copy from SAP standard space SAP_BR_BILLING_CLERK.  By default, there are four pages within this space. Since this space is assigned to a custom business role Z_CREATE_BILLING_DOC, which has one business catalog SAP_SD_BC_BIL_DOC_PC, only limited apps are visible (see my another blog Switching to Spaces and Pages Experience Sharing - Assigning Standard S&P to Your Own Business Roles for further explanation). For example, Page Sales Commissions and Incentives shows no apps; Page Billing Process shows two apps.

 No Apps Shown in Page Sales Commissions and Incentives

 Two Apps Shown in Page Billing Process

By editing this space, I can either Remove or Hide a page to simplify the space.

 Hide a Page in a Space

Here is the final appearance of this custom space with only three apps on one page.

 Appearance of Custom Space ZBR_BILLING_CLERK

 

Best Practice 3 – Checking Changes of SAP Standard Business  Roles/Spaces/Pages at Each Upgrade

At each major upgrade, there might be changes to SAP Standard Business Roles, Spaces and Pages. SAP publishes a SAP Note to address that each time.  For example, for 2402 upgrade, the SAP Note is 3404825 - Identity and Access Management (IAM): Change Overview for SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2402.  In the Note, there is an attachment called Delta_S4CE_BR_240-2308.xlsx, which lists the delta changes from 2308 release.

The Excel file has many worksheets detailing the changes in each area. I am writing a separate blog discussing how to adjust your existing business roles in preparation for the upgrade. Please stay tuned…

 

Best Practice 4 – Designing Business Roles/Spaces/Pages from End User’s Input, Not Excel Sheet. And Keep the Number to the Minimum.

To prepare this Best Practice, I had several discussions with our consultants on the projects.  Besides using SAP standard business roles/spaces/pages, they interviewed end users to decide which one fit to their business needs.  If no standard ones are available, they will design a new one by adapting standard templates to save the time. Each custom business role/space/page was created carefully. The total number should be kept at a minimum.

In contrast, I saw several customers having hundreds of custom business roles. I don’t know if they were carefully designed with end user input one by one, or just extrapolate on an Excel sheet to cover all the scenarios you can think of. As a direct result, I heard some cases that a large portion of custom business roles were not being used, only collecting dust in the system.

 

Best Practice 5 – Following a Good Naming Convention

For the convenience of maintenance and differentiate custom ones from SAP standard ones, we need to establish a systematic naming convention for business roles, spaces and pages.  I have discussed this topic in detail in my blog Naming and Tracking Your Business Roles, Spaces and Pages in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape Systems.  

 

Conclusion

Since customers need to transition to Spaces and Pages, I am outlining five best practices from my involvement with customer projects. The core of the best practices is the cloud mindset, i.e., using SAP predefined Business Role Templates, Spaces and Pages on most occasions. If there are exceptions, adapting these objects is still better than creating new ones from scratch for the sake of long-term maintenance, especially considering we have major upgrades every six months.

 

My Blogs on Spaces and Pages

  • Switching to Spaces and Pages Experience Sharing - Best Practices in Deploying Spaces and Pages (this blog)
  • Switching to Spaces and Pages Experience Sharing - Assigning Standard S&P to Your Own Business Roles
  • Switching to Spaces and Pages Experience Sharing - Using Display Security Audit Log Fiori App
  • Naming and Tracking Your Business Roles, Spaces and Pages in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape Systems
  • Transport Your Spaces and Pages Configurations in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud 3-System Landscape Systems
  • How to Use Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

 

My Colleagues’ Blogs on Spaces and Pages

  • SAP User Experience in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2402
  • Get to Know the New Spaces Concept for SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • Manage Spaces and Pages for SAP Fiori Launchpad
  • How to Efficiently Switch from Groups to Spaces and Pages: Best Practice with Predefined Spaces and Mass Assignment
  • Getting Familiar with Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  • Switching from Groups to Spaces and Pages in SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition

entry.title: Partners Are Vital to Your Company’s Success When Transitioning to Cloud ERP
entry.content:
Are you ready to shift to a modern Cloud ERP solution where you can take advantage of the latest innovations with AI, and ensure security, compliance, and scalability? Or are you holding on to your legacy system and watching your competitors innovate with speed and pass you by?

If you’re ready to start your transformative journey, let me share my insights on the importance of leveraging the right team to support this digital transformation, and how to find it. Before I show you how, I want to point you to a recent post by SAP executive Eric van Rossum, who wrote an article on why the move to a Cloud ERP holds strategic importance for companies around the world, and why it’s important for your company’s success.

In that article, he cautions that companies that fail to modernize their legacy systems risk falling behind competitors who are running their organizations on modern cloud systems. Companies that fail to move to a cloud ERP system clearly don’t have the ability to establish new business models at scale, expand into new markets, adapt, and grow as they are hindered by antiquated systems. In short, they get left behind.

As such, the decision to modernize mission-critical ERP systems doesn’t happen often due to the wide reaching impact on organizations and implementing a new ERP system is a complex endeavor. And you must get it right. And to get it right, you need the right methodology, and you need the right team. In another article, Peter Baskin explained what having the right methodology means, so I encourage you to read that.

Now let’s discuss the second key component to your successful transformation – the right team. Let me explain how you can find the right partner.

 

The Value of the Right Partner

With more than 5,000 customers having experienced this digital transformation, SAP’s partner ecosystem has played an important and powerful role in delivering customer success. Partners support over 90% of customer implementations, giving them the expertise and experience you want when considering the transformation ahead of you.

Your company will likely only go through an ERP implementation once or maybe twice in your career, so leveraging the proven experience of a company that has done it numerous times and has unmatched experience is an absolute must.

After all, you know your business and you excel at it. You live it every day. Similarly, implementation experts know their business. Not only do they excel at what they do, they bring a wealth of knowledge - from product to industry to business process. Their expertise is in delivering successful cloud migration journeys and the great news is that you, too, can leverage their experience.

 

In Any Relationship, Finding the Right Partner is Key

Companies seek a reliable and skillful partner who can show that they understand their business and industry. They are also looking for a partner they can trust to be their technology and business advisor.

On the other side, consulting firms and system integrators can differentiate themselves from their competition through their skills and experience. These firms strive to demonstrate that they have a successful track record, expertise, and work style to meet a customer’s business needs.

SAP has a large ecosystem of over 25,000 partners with different skills, maturity, and experience
to meet the varied needs of organizations. SAP partners are provided with a comprehensive training program to ensure they are current with the latest tools, innovations, and methodologies. Our partners gain credibility in the marketplace by differentiating their skillsets based on their solution capabilities, industry expertise, and project experience.

 

Helping You Identify the Right Partner for Your Needs

Customers require guidance in finding the best-fit partner for their business or industry, a qualified partner, one that is right for their company. To do this a company can leverage the Competency Framework that helps cut through the noise, allowing customers to find the expertise they need – no matter their business challenges, company size, or location.

The Competency Framework recognizes partners who meet the rigorous criteria and requirements of competency with designations that allow them to differentiate their skills, levels of expertise and acumen.

The competency levels (essential, advanced, and expert) and descriptors for each level are displayed below. Use them as a guide for the type of partner that you may need for your specific business needs.

When selecting a partner, SAP encourages customers to leverage the SAP Partner Finder. This platform is designed to help customers discover and connect with partners within our SAP ecosystem to support a successful cloud transformation.

Sample screen from SAP Partner Finder showcasing a partner identified as a “Expert” on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition.

In the partner profile on SAP Partner Finder, you can see a partner’s achieved competencies for SAP S/4HANA Cloud. These achieved competencies are used to provide best-fit partner rankings in SAP Partner Finder search results. This pool of information makes it easier and faster to find the best partner to fit your company’s needs.

SAP Partner Finder will soon have a new filter to search for partners with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition (“RISE with SAP”) and SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (“GROW with SAP”) competencies, making it easier for customers to find the right partner for their Cloud ERP implementations.

 

The Time to Act is NOW

Now that you understand the value of a partner and how to select one, the time to act is now if you are considering transitioning to a cloud ERP solution from SAP.

As part of the RISE with SAP Migration and Modernization program, SAP is offering our customers a transformation incentive (for a limited time) to help offset the cost for new purchases of RISE with SAP or GROW with SAP.

To learn more about the RISE with SAP Migration and Modernization program and the transformation incentive, please join us at the upcoming RISE into the Future event on February 28, where we’ll discuss how to confidently migrate to cloud ERP.
